samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of "size" bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 2*size bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size" value="4">Size parameter for the multiplier.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the input signal 'mul_a' by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extends the input signal 'mul_b' by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_products[0]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the partial product based on the first bit of 'mul_b_ext'. If it's 1, assigns the shifted version of 'mul_a_ext'; otherwise, assigns 0.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_products[1]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the partial product based on the second bit of 'mul_b_ext'. If it's 1, assigns the shifted version of 'mul_a_ext'; otherwise, assigns 0.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_products[2]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the partial product based on the third bit of 'mul_b_ext'. If it's 1, assigns the shifted version of 'mul_a_ext'; otherwise, assigns 0.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_products[3]" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the partial product based on the fourth bit of 'mul_b_ext'. If it's 1, assigns the shifted version of 'mul_a_ext'; otherwise, assigns 0.</assign>
        <assign id="add_regs[0]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the first stage of addition.</assign>
        <assign id="add_regs[1]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the second stage of addition.</assign>
        <assign id="add_regs[2]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the third stage of addition.</assign>
        <assign id="add_regs[3]" width_description="[7:0]">Stores the intermediate sum values for the fourth stage of addition.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Calculates the final product by summing up all the registers.</assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extending inputs
        assign mul_a_ext = {4'b0, mul_a};
        assign mul_b_ext = {4'b0, mul_b};
        
        // Generating partial products
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i=i+1) begin : pp_gen
                if(i == 0) begin
                    assign partial_products[i] = (mul_b_ext[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0;
                end else begin
                    assign partial_products[i] = (mul_b_ext[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0;
                end
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Adding partial products
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j=j+1) begin
                    add_regs[j] <= 0;
                end
            end else begin
                add_regs[0] <= partial_products[0];
                for(j = 1; j < size; j=j+1) begin
                    add_regs[j] <= add_regs[j-1] + partial_products[j];
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Calculating final product
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                mul_out <= 0;
            end else begin
                mul_out <= add_regs[size-1];
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
